sourceMovie.title: District B13
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: District B13 (2004) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Pierre Morel's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Spectral
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If District B13 clicked for you, Spectral (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Logan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If District B13 clicked for you, Logan (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If District B13 clicked for you, Demolition Man (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after District B13?
faq.answer: Try Spectral, Logan, Demolition Man—each shares DNA with District B13 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is District B13?
faq.answer: District B13 (2004) is best described as Action, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is District B13 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "District 13 Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
